summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic RangeCalendar core (issue #287) in the ars-ui Rust project, reusing existing Calendar grid machinery and adding range-specific state, hover preview, span validation, and ARIA attributes.
summary.outcome: RangeCalendar core fully implemented with TDD, comprehensive snapshot/conformance/proptest coverage, spec drift corrected in core and adapter specs, all verification gates passed, and a PR opened closing issue #287 with 31 files changed.

repo: The spec tooling can declare 'No spec change required' but issue-level requirements can override it; when public API contracts (max-span, allow_single_date_range) are missing from the spec, a minimal spec sync is required before implementation.

The pre-push gate is `cargo xci --profile fast` — a 10-step pipeline covering clippy, fmt, unit tests (7,472+), integration, adapter, spec snippets, error variants, mutual-exclusion, and native coverage.

Snapshots live under `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/range_calendar/snapshots/` and the snapshot-count check (step 8 of xci) validates against a per-module budget; RangeCalendar passed at 21 snapshots.

Post-implementation audit is a mandatory project skill that found real spec drift: core/adapter specs described endpoint-only aria-selected and lacked the offset-aware Cell/CellTrigger shape already established by Calendar.
Proptest entries for RangeCalendar compile but are correctly excluded from fast-profile nightly runs — the proptest target name (`proptest_range_calendar`) is the right scope.

code.finding: Grid helpers had to be widened from private to pub(crate) so RangeCalendar could reuse week grid generation, month offsets, visible-range checks, and month stepping without creating a new dependency.
Clippy caught two style issues in the initial implementation: an Option::map().unwrap_or_else() chain and a manual early return that should use ?; both were fixed before the pre-push gate.
RangeCalendar spec conformance coverage added for range_calendar::Part alongside existing Calendar coverage in the shared date_time conformance test file.

Spec drift corrected post-audit: aria-selected must appear on every confirmed in-range cell (not just endpoints), and Cell/CellTrigger anatomy uses the offset-aware shape {date: CalendarDate} consistent with Calendar.

workflow: TDD RED→GREEN: write tests first that fail on missing types, confirm compile-time failures match expectations, then implement — prevents over-building.

`cargo insta test --lib --unreferenced=reject -- range_calendar` fails because the filtered binary still scans unrelated pre-existing unreferenced snapshots across the whole crate; use the unflagged form for the focused module and accept the caveat.
`gh project item-edit` first failed with a local flag difference; the correct invocation uses `--project-id` rather than `--owner --number` when the local gh version differs.

The post-implementation audit skill is a required step for ars-ui agnostic core work — spec drift (aria-selected scope, anatomy shape) was caught and landed in the same PR rather than as follow-up debt.
PRs with `.snap` files require the `snapshot-reviewed` label before merge.

friction: `gh project item-edit` CLI flag mismatch between local gh version and expected flags; required switching from `--owner/--number` form to `--project-id` form.

`cargo insta test --lib --unreferenced=reject -- range_calendar` incorrectly fails because the filtered test binary scans all existing snapshots in the crate, not just the targeted module's; no clean per-module unreferenced check is available.

Spec declared 'No spec change required' for issue #287 but the issue itself specified two public options (`allow_single_date_range`, `min_range_days`, `max_range_days`) absent from the spec, requiring a planning-phase decision to reconcile the conflict.
`cargo xci --profile fast` has substantial runtime (7,472+ unit tests + coverage instrumentation build); the coverage phase alone requires a full incremental rebuild with instrumentation.
open_items: The `--unreferenced=reject` caveat with filtered insta runs has no clean per-module solution; the full workspace unreferenced check should be run separately or the insta CLI needs a module-scoped mode.
PR still needs the `snapshot-reviewed` label applied after opening (21 new RangeCalendar snapshots).
Codex review loop (`waiting-for-codex-review` skill) was not yet started at transcript end — CI green and Codex 👍 are still pending.
Leptos and Dioxus adapter components for RangeCalendar are explicitly out of scope for this PR; adapter work is deferred to a follow-up issue.
